META.reasoning: No valid breakout setup exists for META. The stock suffered a violent post-earnings breakdown on Apr 30, collapsing ~$57 from $669.12 to $611.91 close (intraday low $600.00) on 3.34x relative volume — institutional distribution, not a breakout catalyst. Per the wiki, the prior $675-677 breakout thesis is fully invalidated. Key issues for breakout strategy: (1) No consolidation range or base is forming — price is in active post-earnings trending decline; (2) Bollinger Band width is 0.2353 (daily) and 0.1434 (hourly) — bands are expanding post-breakdown, not squeezing; no coiled-spring setup; (3) ADX 49.21 with minus_di (34.51) > plus_di (26.27) confirms a strong bearish trending regime, not a range-bound environment that precedes breakouts; (4) Hourly ADX minus_di at 49.52 vs plus_di at 8.98 is extreme directional imbalance — active freefall, not consolidation; (5) Support at $627.61 was breached by the gap-down; the $600 hourly pivot held intraday but the stock did not close below it, so no confirmed downside breakout to trade either; (6) Nearest resistance is $618-630 dead-cat bounce zone (prior support turned resistance, Fib 38.2% at $626.10) — this is a mean-reversion target, not a breakout level; (7) Wiki warns next entry only on bounce into $618-630 before considering a short. For a bearish breakout entry to be valid, I would need: price to consolidate above $600 support for several sessions, BB width to contract back into a squeeze, then a high-volume close below $600. That setup does not exist today. Recent lesson (NVDA breakout stop triggered at Fib 61.8%) reinforces discipline around premature breakout entries in volatile post-event tape. Range boundaries identified: upper range $627-630 (resistance), lower range $600 (support). Volume ratio: 3.34x (distribution, not breakout accumulation). Squeeze status: off — already fired to the downside. No actionable setup for breakout strategy.
META.timeframe: no setup — revisit in 5-10 trading days once consolidation forms

META.reasoning: Two of five agents are explicitly bearish (swing_mean_reversion at 68 confidence, swing_catalyst_news at 78 confidence); three are neutral (swing_trend_momentum at 82, swing_breakout at 22, swing_macro_context at 38). On raw vote count this looks like a 3-neutral / 2-bearish split, but the neutral calls are context-conditional — every neutral agent is neutral because no entry exists *right now* at $611-613, not because they are bullish. All five agents converge on the same picture: META suffered a post-earnings institutional distribution collapse (-7.9% on April 30, 3.34x average volume), the prior $675-677 breakout thesis is fully invalidated, and price is below ALL EMAs on daily and hourly. The three neutrals are waiting for a dead-cat bounce to $618-630 before acting; the two bearish agents plan to short that same bounce. This is functional agreement dressed as disagreement: when the bounce materializes, 4-of-5 agents effectively shift to bearish. Macro context is risk-on (S&P/Nasdaq at record highs) but swing_macro_context applies a stock-specific veto: the capex shock narrative is decoupled from the broad market tailwind and the asymmetric R/R at current levels is negative (<1:1 for longs, <2:1 for shorts). A short setup only qualifies once price bounces to the $626-630 resistance cluster (Fib 38.2%, broken prior support), where the R/R improves to approximately 3:1 (entry ~$628, target ~$583, stop ~$643). Wiki memory reinforces caution: the only recent comparable setup — EMA pullback Fib dip-buy on NVDA — produced a -$264.65 loss (0% win rate on this setup-type in the last 30 days), confirming that 'oversold AI name post-catalyst' longs are a losing pattern. META-specific trade history shows no open position and two prior closed/abandoned longs at $673-677 both resulted in losses — no long-bias recency bias to overcome. Recommended action: no trade until a bounce to $626-630 is confirmed intraday; then short with entry ~$628, target ~$585 (Fib 61.8%), stop ~$643. If price breaks below $600 support on volume without a bounce, re-evaluate for a momentum continuation short entry below that level.
META.key_conflicts: The primary conflict is swing_trend_momentum (neutral, 82 confidence) vs swing_mean_reversion and swing_catalyst_news (both bearish). Trend_momentum is neutral because its strategy rules require price to be ABOVE EMAs with accelerating ROC — those conditions are absent, so it outputs 'no setup' rather than a directional call. Mean_reversion is bearish because it reads the same oversold conditions as a dead-cat bounce opportunity to fade, not a dip to buy. This is the classic 'trend_momentum bearish + mean_reversion bearish = trend extended, NOT counter-trend opportunity' pattern from the prompt: both are pointing the same direction by different paths. The genuine conflict is swing_macro_context (neutral): it acknowledges the broad macro is risk-on but refuses to call a short until asymmetric R/R is present (requires the bounce). This is a timing disagreement, not a directional one — macro_context agrees the direction is bearish but vetoes a trade at current prices on R/R grounds. Swing_breakout is the odd signal: confidence 22 (lowest of all agents), outputs neutral with entry/target/stop all set to $611.91 (placeholder) — effectively abstaining because no coiled-spring setup exists. Its low confidence prevents it from distorting the synthesis. The only genuine directional disagreement is swing_catalyst_news placing the short entry at $630 vs swing_mean_reversion at $619 — a $11 spread on entry. These cluster tightly enough (both within the $618-633 resistance band) to use the median ($624-625) or wait for a confirmed intraday reversal candle in that zone before shorting.
META.condition: NO TRADE until price bounces to $626-630 intraday and shows a confirmed rejection candle (bearish engulfing or pin bar on hourly). If price drops straight through $600 support on volume without a bounce, reassess for a continuation short below $597.

META.reasoning: TREND: Daily EMA alignment technically shows 10 EMA (656) > 21 EMA (647) > 50 EMA (639), but this is a lagging artifact — current price at 611.91 has broken decisively below ALL EMAs (5/10/21/50/200), trading -5.39% below 21 EMA and -4.23% below 50 EMA. This is not a pullback to EMA; it is a confirmed breakdown through the entire EMA stack. ADX at 49.21 is strong, but minus_DI (34.51) exceeds plus_DI (26.27), confirming the dominant trend force is now bearish sellers, not buyers. Hourly EMAs are not aligned in either direction, and price is below all hourly EMAs as well — no multi-timeframe confirmation of an uptrend. MOMENTUM: Deeply negative and decelerating. ROC 5d: -7.17%, ROC 10d: -9.60% — both sharply negative. ROC 21d is still +6.95% but is being rapidly dragged down. MACD histogram on daily: -2.16 (negative). Hourly MACD histogram: -8.46 (deeply negative, momentum accelerating to the downside). RSI 7 at 23.55 and hourly RSI 21 at 24.16 signal extreme short-term oversold conditions, but oversold does not equal bullish trend+momentum setup. Volume on the breakdown day (52.67M vs 20D avg 15.78M, 3.34x relative volume) confirms institutional distribution. WIKI CONTEXT: Q1 2026 earnings (Apr 29 AH) triggered a ~10% single-session collapse from $669 to ~$601-603 on 9.99x relative hourly volume — institutional distribution confirmed. The prior April 15-17 breakout thesis at $675-677 is fully invalidated. Wiki flags next actionable entry only on a dead-cat bounce into $618-630 resistance before considering a short. Current price (~$611-613) is just below that resistance band, which reinforces caution rather than bullishness. LESSONS: Recent EMA pullback/Fib dip-buy trade on NVDA lost -$264.65 (0% win rate in last 30 days on this setup type); swing_trend_momentum was bullish in that losing trade under similar conditions (price below EMAs, oversold RSI, AI thesis intact) — lowering confidence in any bullish entry here. CONCLUSION: Strategy requires both trend AND momentum ACCELERATING. Neither condition is met for a bullish setup. The breakdown is post-earnings institutional selling with no trend+momentum confirmation. The oversold readings may attract a bounce, but a bounce into $618-630 would be resistance, not a clean EMA pullback entry in an intact uptrend. Outputting neutral per strategy rules: no entry when momentum is decelerating and price is below all EMAs.
META.timeframe: wait — no setup active; re-evaluate if price reclaims 21 EMA (~647) with expanding ROC and MACD histogram turning positive

META.reasoning:
1. MACRO REGIME: Broadly risk-on — S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed April at record highs (+9% MTD, best since 2020), Fed held at 3.5-3.75% (third consecutive hold), AI capex cycle intact (Alphabet and Amazon Q1 expansion confirmed). However, META is in a stock-specific bear regime driven by a capex shock (full-year guidance raised to $125-145B vs expected discipline), which is decoupled from the broad market tailwind. The macro regime supports mega-cap tech generally but does NOT offset a single-name fundamental reversal of this magnitude. Iran blockade tail risk intact as left-tail overlay.

2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: Underperforming. META is below ALL daily and hourly moving averages (EMA-5, -10, -20, -50, -200; all short-term SMAs). April 30 saw a -7.9% single-day collapse on 3.34x relative volume (52.67M vs 15.78M avg), confirming institutional distribution. ADX at 49.21 with -DI 34.51 >> +DI 26.27 signals a strong confirmed downtrend. OBV trending down, volume bias 10d bearish. While the broad market rallied in April, META collapsed after earnings — severe relative underperformance. Hourly hourly RSI-21 at 24.16, daily RSI-7 at 23.55: both deeply oversold, but oversold can stay oversold in distribution phases.

3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: At current price ($613), neither direction meets minimum 2:1. LONG bounce play: entry $613, target $627 (dead-cat to Fib 38.2% per wiki thesis), stop below $597 (volume-confirmed hourly support at $600 minus $3 buffer) = $14 upside / $16 downside = 0.875:1 — REJECTED, negative R/R. SHORT at current levels: entry $613, target $583 (wiki medium-term bias), stop $638 (above 10% bounce zone) = $30 downside / $25 stop risk = 1.2:1 — REJECTED, below 2:1 minimum. A superior short entry would materialize IF price bounces to $626-630 (Fib 38.2%): entry $628, target $583, stop $643 → $45 gain / $15 stop = 3.0:1 — passes the 3:1 threshold. That setup does not exist yet.

4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: Balance sheet is strong (no leverage concerns). Volatility is elevated (ATR 3.22% daily, 1.32% hourly) but manageable. The real risk is that the capex shock narrative extends: if Q2 earnings guidance includes further AI infrastructure spending, the next leg down could accelerate. The wiki thesis explicitly flags the prior bull trigger (capex discipline) has been negated — this is now a 'fallen angel' scenario where consensus upgrades have not yet cleared. Macro regime being risk-on creates a false sense of safety; META is diverging from sector.

5. LESSONS/PATTERNS: The lessons file shows a recent EMA pullback Fib dip-buy on NVDA failed (-$264.65) amid stretched macro RSI. The pattern of 'dip buy on AI name' is 0-for-1 in the last 30 days. This reinforces caution on initiating a bounce long in META despite oversold RSI readings.

SUMMARY: Macro is risk-on but META is in a stock-specific bear cycle. Current price offers no asymmetric entry point in either direction. Wait for either (a) a bounce to $626-630 to initiate a short with 3:1+ R/R targeting $583, or (b) a confirmed stabilization candle above $627 on volume for a potential dead-cat long. Neutral now; reassess on bounce.

Branch B fires technically (price ~$611.91 sits between Fib 38.2% at $626.10 and 50% at $605.89 from the $691.52–$520.26 swing), but the setup is a post-earnings structural breakdown, not a healthy trend pullback — Branch B dip-buy is disqualified. Branch A (fade the extreme) provides the actionable thesis, but inverted: the RSI-7 (23.55) and hourly RSI-21 (24.16) are deeply oversold, hourly Bollinger %B is -0.0083 (outside lower band), and daily RSI divergence is bullish — all signaling an imminent dead-cat bounce. The trade is to FADE that bounce into resistance, not buy the dip. ADX 49.21 with -DI 34.51 >> +DI 26.27 confirms a strong downtrend in force. OBV is trending down on both daily and hourly. The April 30th sell-off was 3.34x average volume (9.99x relative hourly per wiki), consistent with institutional distribution after Q1 2026 earnings capex shock. Prior support $627.61 (11 tests) was broken and is now resistance. Wiki context (non-stale, 2026-04-30) explicitly flags: bearish medium-term bias, dead-cat bounce to $618–630 expected, then consider short. Lessons table shows 0% win rate on EMA pullback dip-buy setups in the last 30 days (NVDA stop-out), reinforcing the decision to avoid a long. Confidence docked from 75 to 68 because: (1) the hourly bullish divergence has not yet fired (RSI divergence false on hourly), meaning the bounce may not materialize immediately; (2) this is a third-touch-type scenario at the lower BB region which lowers fade conviction per wiki guidance. Plan: enter short on the expected dead-cat bounce into $618–630 resistance cluster, target $590, stop above $633 (above broken support now resistance).

CATALYST WINDOW: Q1 2026 earnings fired 1 trading day ago (April 29 AH) — we are inside the 1-3 day post-catalyst re-rating window. This is the dominant catalyst. NET NEWS SENTIMENT: NEGATIVE. EPS beat 57% and revenue beat +33% YoY were overshadowed by a massive capex guidance raise to $125-145B (nearly double FY2025's $72B), confirming the bear case thesis of free cash flow compression. DAP growth slowed to +4% YoY / down >5% QoQ. 8,000 layoffs (10% of workforce) announced — signals cost pressure is real. Social media addiction litigation (California 70% liability verdict, 10,000+ lawsuits, $375M New Mexico award) remains a persistent high-negative overhang. Muse Spark AI narrative losing ground vs Alphabet's stronger AI monetization story. Some analysts moved to Hold post-Q1 with $700-range targets. INSIDER ACTIVITY: Quiet — no insider trades in the last 30 days. No insider buying to suggest confidence ahead of earnings. TECHNICAL SETUP (poor/broken — not constructive): Price gapped down from $669.12 to $611.91 on 3.34x average volume — heavy institutional distribution. Price is below ALL moving averages on both daily and hourly timeframes. Daily ADX 49.21 with -DI (34.51) dominating +DI (26.27): strong confirmed downtrend. Hourly ADX 30.67, -DI 49.52 — even stronger bearish trend on shorter timeframe. OBV trending down, volume bias 10d: bearish. Hourly Bollinger pct_b at -0.0083 (at/below lower band). Daily RSI-7 at 23.55 and hourly RSI-21 at 24.16 indicate oversold conditions — a dead-cat bounce is possible — but daily RSI divergence (bullish: true) has NOT been confirmed with a price follow-through. MACD remains in deep negative territory on both timeframes. ROC-5d: -7.17%, ROC-10d: -9.6% — momentum firmly bearish. LESSONS CHECK: Recent EMA pullback Fib dip-buy on NVDA failed (-$264.65, stop triggered at hourly Fib 61.8%); a similar dip-buy thesis here would be analogous and risky given the stronger negative catalyst. STRATEGY: Short entry on a relief bounce toward the earnings-gap breakdown zone ($630-635), targeting Fib 61.8% at $585.68 (daily). Stop above the gap-fill zone at $645 to limit risk if institutions step in for a full gap fill. No binary catalyst within the next 10 trading days (Q2 2026 earnings expected late July) — this is a post-earnings multiple compression / institutional re-rating drift play. Confidence reduced from 85 to 78 due to extreme oversold RSI (bounce risk) and large bid at $600 hourly pivot (volume-confirmed support).
